The Water Supply (Water Quality) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Water Supply (Water Quality) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 (the “1990 Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Britannia Cablesystems Solent Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Britannia Cablesystems Solent Limited on 9th April 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the City of Portsmouth, the whole of the Boroughs of Fareham, Gosport and Havant, part of the Borough of Eastleigh and part of the District of East Hampshire. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 12th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Britannia Cablesystems Solent Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 10th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Clyde Cablevision) (Paisley and Renfrew) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Clyde Cablevision on 29th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the northern part of the District of Renfrew, including the towns of Paisley, Renfrew, Erskine, Barrhead, Johnstone and Bridge of Weir. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 12th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Clyde Cablevision will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Paisley and Renfrew public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 10th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Telford Telecommunications Limited) Order 1991
The Premium Savings Bonds (Amendment No.2) Regulations 1991
The Farm and Conservation Grant (Variation) Scheme 1991 (revoked)
The Farm Diversification Grant (Variation) (No. 2) Scheme 1991
The Bitton Light Railway Order 1991
The County Court (Forms) (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1991
The Merchant Shipping (Radio and Radio-Navigational Equipment Survey) Regulations 1991
The Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) (Amendment) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules amend the Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) Rules 1975. They provide for surveys of radio equipment on fishing vessels carried out in the United Kingdom to be carried out by surveyors appointed by British Telecom. Such surveys if carried out elsewhere may be done by British Telecom surveyors or surveyors of the Department of Transport.

items.title: Food Hygiene (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Electricity (Standards of Performance) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the sum which a supplier must pay to a customer by way of compensation for failure to meet specified standards of performance in respect of the electricity supply services to be provided by public electricity suppliers (“suppliers”). The sum payable differs between domestic and non-domestic customers, and between standards. The standards do not apply to customers supplied under special agreements.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Experimental Traffic (Amendment) Order 1991
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Experimental Traffic (Amendment) Order 1991
The National Health Service Trusts (Consultation on Dissolution) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the consultation to be completed before the Secretary of State may make an order dissolving an NHS trust under paragraph 29 of Schedule 2 to the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. In cases where the reason forthe dissolution is the creation of a new NHS trust, regulation 1(3) makes it clear that the consultation prescribed on dissolution may be combined with the consultation required by the Act before the establishment of an NHS trust.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) Amendment Regulations 1991
The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations”), which provide for the arrangements under which dentists provide general dental services as part of the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Registration) Modification Rules Approved Order 1991
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, and made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting (“the Council”), modify in certain cases the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 so as to reduce from 3 years to six months the period for which a person may remain registered as a nurse, midwife or health visitor (“practitioner”) before she becomes liable to pay a fee for renewing her registration. The reduced registration period applies only in the case of a practitioner whose previously ineffective registration is again made effective following her application to the Council for the purpose, provided that the application is accompanied by a declaration to the effect that she proposes to practise only in response to a need for additional practitioners which arises out of the present hostilities in the Gulf region.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Clyde Cablevision) (Greater Glasgow) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Clyde Cablevision on 29th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in all but the north-western part of the City of Glasgow and small adjoining parts of the Districts of Strathkelvin and Eastwood. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 13th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Clyde Cablevision will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Greater Glasgow public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 11th July 1991.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Britannia Cablesystems Darlington Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Britannia Cablesystems Darlington Limited on 18th March 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Borough of Darlington. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 13th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Britannia Cablesystems Darlington Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 11th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Clyde Cablevision) (Bearsden and Milngavie) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Clyde Cablevision on 29th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in all but the north-western part of the District of Bearsden and Milngavie. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Clyde Cablevision will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Bearsden and Milngavie public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 15th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Clyde Cablevision) (Inverclyde) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Clyde Cablevision on 29th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the northern part of the District of Inverclyde, including the towns of Greenock, Gourock, Port Glasgow and Kilmalcolm. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 18th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Clyde Cablevision will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Inverclyde public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 16th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Diamond Cable (Melton Mowbray) Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Diamond Cable (Melton Mowbray) Limited on 27th March 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Melton Mowbray area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 18th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Diamond Cable (Melton Mowbray) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 16th July 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Videotron Harrow Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Telecable of Harrow Limited (“the Company”) on 9th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the London Borough of Harrow. The Company changed its name to Videotron Harrow Limited on 11th March 1991. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th June 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Videotron Harrow Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 15th July 1991.

items.title: The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1991
The Merchant Shipping Act 1970 (Unregistered Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1991
The Merchant Shipping Act 1970 (Unregistered Ships) Regulations 1991
The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (unregistered Ships) Regulations 1991
The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment No.3) Regulations 1991
The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1991
items.description: This Order, which revokes the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1990, makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and other obligations relating to sea fishing set out in the Regulation adopted by the Council of the European Communities on 20th December 1990 (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation fixes total allowable catches and quotas for 1991.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Specified Sea Areas) (Regulation of Nets and Other Fishing Gear) Order 1991 (revoked)
The Blue Eared Pig Disease Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for controls in relation to Blue Eared Pig Disease.

items.title: The Price Marking Order 1991
items.description: This Order implements European Community Council Directive 79/581/EEC (OJ No. L 158, 26.6.79, p.19), as amended by Council Directive 88/315/EEC (OJ No. L 142, 9.6.88, p.23), and Council Directive 88/314/EEC (OJ No. L 142, 9.6.88, p.19) on consumer protection in the indication of the prices of foodstuffs and non-food products respectively.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No. 3) Revocation Order 1991
The Sea Fishing (Days in Port) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the enforcement throughout the United Kingdom of Article 13 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3926/90, fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks, the total allowable catches for 1991 and certain conditions under which they may be fished (OJ No. L378, 31.12.90, p. 1).

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Application of Section 122 to Institutions in Wales) Order 1991
The Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1991
items.description: This Order replaces the Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1989. The Order specifies descriptions and classes of medicinal products (being veterinary drugs) for the purposes of section 58 of the Medicines Act 1968. By virtue of section 58 such products may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by a veterinary surgeon or veterinary practitioner and may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a person.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for further, miscellaneous, amendments to the Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) Order 1989.

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Children Act 1989) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules are made as a consequence of the passing of the Children Act 1989 (c. 41) and set out the procedures to be followed in family proceedings courts in public and private law proceedings in which any question with respect to a child arises.

items.title: The Eggs (Marketing Standards) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Eggs (Marketing Standards) Regulations 1985 following the replacement of the applicable European Community provisions (regulation 5). They apply throughout Great Britain and come into force on 1st July 1991, although one provision of the applicable Council Regulation, dealing with the disposal of eggs broken in packing centres, does not, by its terms, come into force until 1st January 1992.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers Rules) 1991
items.description: This version of the Act of Sederunt (Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers Rules) 1991 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government (Committees and Political Groups) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government (Committees and Political Groups) Regulations 1990 which made provision for securing the political balance on certain relevant authorities, committees and bodies and for determining the voting rights of members on certain committees.

items.title: The Crop Residues (Restrictions on Burning) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, impose restrictions on burning of certain crop residues on agricultural land (regulation 3). The crop residues to which the Regulations apply (Schedule 1) are:

items.title: The Community Charges (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings parts of section 16 of the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 (which relates to applications for licences) into force on 8th July 1991 for the purpose of fixing the initial fee payable with licence applications, and imposing the requirement that licence applications be made in an approved form and accompanied by the initial fee. Section 48(1) (which provides for the Act to extend to Northern Ireland) is also brought into force on the same date so far as it relates to those provisions of section 16.

items.title: The Erskine Bridge Tolls Extension Order 1991
The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) (Welsh Forms and Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: Mae'r Rheoliadau hyn yn diwygio'r Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) (Welsh Forms and Particulars) Regulations 1991 (“y prif Reoliadau”). Mae'r diwygiadau'n gyfieithiad i'r Gymraeg o'r diwygiadau a wnaed gan yr Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1991 i'r Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Prescribed Forms and Particulars) Regulations 1990. Mae Rheoliad 2(2) yn cywiro gwall ym mhennawd Ffurflen 2 y prif Reoliadau.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1991 consequent on the delegation of radio survey work to British Telecommunications plc by the Merchant Shipping (Radio and Radio-Navigational Equipment Survey) Regulations 1991 and the Fishing Vessels (Safety Provisions) (Amendment) Rules 1991. The fees for the work involved leading to the issue of a certificate (including the issue itself) is to be charged at an hourly rate of £44, together with a fixed fee of £72 for fishing vessels, and £88 for other ships, in place of an hourly rate only of £55. Where the survey is performed abroad by a locally appointed surveyor, a separate charge at the hourly rate is made for the issue of a certificate.

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Constitution) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules, which do not apply in the inner London area or in the City of London, are made as a consequence of the introduction, by the Children Act 1989 (c. 41), of family proceedings in magistrates' courts which, from 14th October 1991, replace domestic proceedings and thereby create a need for family panels to be constituted in place of domestic court panels.

items.title: The Farm Business Non-Capital Grant (Variation) Scheme 1991
The Savings Certificates (Children’s Bonus Bonds) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations enable the Director of Savings to issue national savings certificates under the name of children’s bonus bonds. They reproduce the provisions of other Regulations made under section 11 of the National Debt Act 1972, the most recent being the Savings Certificates Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/1031), with the following main differences:

items.title: The Broadcasting (Independent Productions) Order 1991
items.description: This Order defines the expressions “qualifying programmes” and “independent productions” for the purposes of section 16(2)(h) of the Broadcasting Act 1990 (requirements with which a regional Channel 3 service should comply). The definitions are also applied by section 25(4) of that Act (Channel 4), section 29(2) (Channel 5), section 44(4)(b) (domestic satellite service) and section 186(2)(a) (the British Broadcasting Corporation).

items.title: The Water (Compulsory Works Powers) (Notice) Regulations 1991
items.description: Paragraph 1(1)(c) of Schedule 20 to the Water Act 1989 requires the National Rivers Authority and water undertakers to serve notice on certain persons when they apply for an order under section 155 of the Act conferring compulsory works powers.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Local Lists) (Amendment and Miscellaneous Provision) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Local Lists) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”) and make further provision in connection with non-domestic rates.

items.title: The County Council of Humberside (River Hull Tunnel) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1991
The Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966 (Schedule 3 Amendment) Order 1991
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.6) (Discharge of Judicial Factors) 1991
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session by making provision for the discharge of certain appointments of judicial factors by application in writing to the Accountant of Court instead of by petition to the court.

items.title: The Emergency Protection Order (Transfer of Responsibilities) Regulations 1991
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (Revocation) Order 1991
The Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Central Lists) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Central Lists) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”).

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Constitution) (Metropolitan Area) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules, which only apply in the inner London area and the City of London, are made as a consequence of the introduction, by the Children Act 1989, of family proceedings in magistrates' courts which, from 14th October 1991, replace domestic proceedings and thereby create a need for family panels to be constituted in place of domestic court panels.

items.title: The Education (University Commissioners) Order 1991
The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Amendment) Rules 1991
The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1991
The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Improvement between Silver Street and Hall Lane, Supplementary Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Improvement between Silver Street and Hall Lane Trunk Roads, Slip Roads and Bridge) Order 1991
Act of Sederunt (Applications under Part VII of the Companies Act 1989) 1991
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for the manner of applications for certain orders or directions under Part VII of the Companies Act 1989 (Financial Markets and Insolvency) and for the manner and circumstances in which notice should be given to interested persons.

items.title: The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 11) Order 1991
items.description: The Order brings sections 35 and 36 of the Companies Act 1989 into force on 26th June 1991. Section 35 obliges the Secretary of State to make regulations requiring the keeping of a register of auditors. Section 36 obliges the Secretary of State to make regulations requiring recognised supervisory bodies under Part II of the Companies Act 1989 to keep and make available to the public certain information relating to bodies corporate and partnerships eligible for appointment as company auditor.

items.title: The Education (Inner London Education Authority) (Transfer of Functions) Order 1991
items.description: This Order confers on the London Residuary Body the statutory duties conferred on the Inner London Education Authority (“ILEA”) under Schedule 10 to the Education Reform Act 1988 to arrive with the Education Assets Board at such written agreements, and to execute such other instruments, as are necessary or expedient to identify or define property, rights and liabilities the subject of sections 74, 126 and 130 of the Education Reform Act 1988 (transfer of property to grant-maintained schools, higher education corporations and designated institutions). However, where such functions relate to or affect property, rights or liabilities transferred to an inner London council under section 168 of that Act they are instead transferred to that council. In certain circumstances the inner London council is obliged to consult the London Residuary Body before exercising such functions.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1991
The Financial Assistance (Sewerage Improvements) (Scotland) Order 1991
The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Criminal Justice (International Co-operation) Act 1990 (Enforcement of Overseas Forfeiture Orders) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement in England and Wales of orders made by a court in a designated country or territory for the forfeiture and destruction or other disposal of property used in connection with the commission of a drug trafficking offence. These powers will also apply to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (International Co-operation) Act 1990 (Enforcement of Overseas Forfeiture Orders) (Northern Ireland) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement in Northern Ireland of orders made by a court in a designated country or territory for the forfeiture and destruction or other disposal of property used in connection with the commission of a drug trafficking offence. These powers will also apply to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there.

items.title: The Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 (Designated Countries and Territories) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 (Designated Countries and Territories) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1199) which provides that, subject to certain modifications, the Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 applies to an order made by a court in a designated country or territory for the purpose of recovering payments or other rewards received in connection with drug trafficking or their value, and to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there.

items.title: The Confiscation of the Proceeds of Drug Trafficking (Designated Countries and Territories) (Scotland) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides that, subject to certain modifications, Part I of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 applies to an order made by a court in a designated country or territory for the purpose of recovering payments or other rewards received in connection with drug trafficking or their value (an external confiscation order), and to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there. It also provides, in Article 8, that the value of any property recovered in a designated country or territory in response to a request by the government of the United Kingdom for assistance in the enforcement of a confiscation order abroad is to be treated as reducing the amount payable in Scotland under a confiscation order made by the High Court.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (International Co-operation) Act 1990 (Enforcement of Overseas Forfeiture Orders) (Scotland) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement in Scotland of orders made by a court in a designated country or territory for the forfeiture and destruction or other disposal of property used in connection with the commission of a drug trafficking offence. These powers will also apply to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Categorisation of Registries of Overseas Territories) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1991
items.description: This Order substitutes a new Class XVE in Schedule 1 to the Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Order 1981 following the enactment of the Electricity Act 1989 and Regulations made thereunder.

items.title: The Gas (Meters) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Finance Act 1985 (Interest on Tax) (Prescribed Rate) (No.3) Order 1991
The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order prohibits, subject to an exception, fishing for cod (Gadus morhua) during the period beginning with the date of coming into force of the Order and ending immediately before 1st September 1991–

items.title: The Medicines (Products for Human Use — Fees) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the fees which are to be payable under the Medicines Act 1971 in respect of licences and certificates relating to medicinal products for human use granted under the Medicines Act 1968.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Food Safety (Exports) Regulations 1991
The Welfare of Pigs Regulations 1991
The Parental Responsibility Agreement Regulations 1991
The North Surrey Water Company (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1991
items.description: Section 101(2) of the water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company.  The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (England) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the form and contents of community charge demand notices, and for the contents of rate demand notices, which are issued by charging authorities in England (other than the Common Council of the City of London), and for the information to be supplied with such notices. The Regulations have effect in relation to demand notices issued with respect to financial years beginning on or after 1st April 1991.

items.title: The Winchester–Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Newbury Bypass) Order 1991
The Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Newbury Bypass Detrunking) Order 1991
The Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Newbury Bypass) Slip Roads Order 1991
The Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (A34) (Newbury Bypass) Slip Roads (No 2) Order 1991
The Advisory Committee on Hazardous Substances Order 1991
The Advisory Committee on Hazardous Substances (Terms of Office) Regulations 1991
The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (City of London) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the contents of community charge demand notices and rate demand notices issued by the Common Council of the City of London, and for the information to be supplied with such notices. The Regulations have effect in relation to demand notices issued with respect to financial years beginning on or after 1st April 1991.

items.title: The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Regulations 1989 (the principal Regulations) to make aided places available to choristers, to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants under the aided places scheme, and in certain other respects.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (the principal Regulations) to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants under the assisted places scheme and in certain other minor aspects.

items.title: The Immigration (Carriers' Liability Prescribed Sum) Order 1991
The International Fund for Agricultural Development (Third Replenishment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the payment to the International Fund for Agricultural Development of additional sums not exceeding £11,305,433 as representing the contribution of the Government of the United Kingdom to the Third Replenishment of the resources of the Fund, and for the redemption of non-interest-bearing and non-negotiable notes issued by the Secretary of State in payment of the contribution. The Order further provides that certain sums which may be received by the Government of the United Kingdom from the Fund shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund. The provisions of Resolution 56/Xll may be obtained by application to the Overseas Development Administration, Eland House, Stag Place, London SWIE 5DH.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Videotron South Limited) (Winchester) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Videotron South Limited on 22nd April 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the City of Winchester. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 3rd July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Videotron South Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Winchester public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 31st July 1991. A copy of the licence may be obtained from the Office of Telecommunications, Export House, 50 Ludgate Hill, London EC4M 7JJ.

items.title: The Children (Secure Accommodation) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations replace the Secure Accommodation (No. 2) Regulations 1983 and the Secure Accommodation (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1986 in consequence of the bringing into force of section 25 of the Children Act 1989 and the repeal of section 21A of the Child Care Act 1980 (c. 5) which it replaces. The main changes include the extension of the secure accommodation provisions to children accommodated by health or local education authorities or National Health Service trusts or in residential care, nursing or mental nursing homes. The Regulations also prohibit for the first time the use of voluntary homes and registered children’s homes for restricting the liberty of children.

items.title: Children’s Homes Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations supersede with modifications the existing regulations relating to children’s homes, whether community homes, registered children’s homes or voluntary homes.

items.title: The Refuges (Children’s Homes and Foster Placements) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of registered children’s homes and voluntary homes which are used as refuges and foster parents who provide refuges in accordance with section 51 of the Children Act 1989. Regulation 3 provides for the requirements which are to be complied with while a certificate is in force andregulation 4 makes provision for the withdrawal of certificates by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Education (School Teacher Appraisal) Regulations 1991
The Prosecution of Offences (Custody Time Limits) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Schedule 1 (Amendment) and Miscellaneous Exemption) Order 1991
The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 “the principal Regulations”), and amend transitional provisions in the Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”).

items.title: The Building Societies Act 1986 (Continuance of section 41) Order 1991
The Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1991
The Returning Officers (Parliamentary Constituencies) (England and Wales) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Family Credit (General) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 in the following respects–

items.title: The Rent Regulation (Forms and Information etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with amendments various Regulations dealing with forms and information relating to certain kinds of tenancies. The principal change is the introduction of new types of form used in connection with applications for registration of a rent or for a certificate of fair rent under a regulated tenancy or under a tenancy where the landlord is a registered housing association. The Regulations also increase from 35p to £1.50 the fee for a certified copy of an entry in the register of rents.

items.title: The Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with only minor and drafting amendments the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) and subsequent amending instruments. They enable the Secretary of State to pay allowances to persons attending courses of education who fulfil certain criteria as to eligibility and they prescribe the conditions and requirements subject to which allowances may be paid.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy Apparatus (Low Power Devices) (Exemption) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the exemption from the provision of section 1(1) of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 of various low power devices. Accordingly it will not be necessary to hold a licence to instal and use apparatus to which these Regulations apply. The descriptions of low power devices which are exempt are set out in the Schedule.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1991
The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 1) Regulations 1991
The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1991
The Tobacco Products Labelling (Safety) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 89/622/EEC concerning the labelling of tobacco products (OJ No. L359 8.12.89 p 1). The Regulations are made in exercise of powers contained in the Consumer Protection Act 1987 and in the European Communities Act 1972.

items.title: The Control of Explosives Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make new provision for controllling the acquisition and keeping of explosives, replacing the instruments revoked by regulation 19(3).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No.4) Order 1991
The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988. The main changes are–

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seed Potatoes (Fees) Regulations 1990. They prescribe increased fees in respect of certain matters arising under the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1984 (S.I. 1984/412 amended by S.I. 1987/547, 1988/1759) (regulation 2).

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1974 (Interim Period Extension) Order 1991
The Public Works Loans (Fees) Regulations 1991
The Human Fertilisation and Embryology (Statutory Storage Period) Regulations 1991
The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Large Goods and Passenger-Carrying Vehicles) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1991
The North-West of Doncaster-Kendal Trunk Road (Airedale Route) (Bingley to Cottingley Bar Section and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The North-West of Doncaster—Kendal Trunk Road (Airedale Route—Bingley to Kildwick and Link Road) Order 1982 Variation (No. 2) Order 1991
The North-West of Doncaster-Kendal Trunk Road (Airedale Route) (Crossflatts to Cottingley Bar Section) (De-Trunking) Order 1991
The Immigration Appeals (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules 1991
The A5 Trunk Road (Fazeley Two Gates Wilnecote Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 4 Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 in the following respects—

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Essex) (No. 2) Order 1991
The British Shipbuilders Regulations 1991
The A40 Trunk Road (Gipsy Corner Junction Improvement, Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The A40 Trunk Road (Western Circus Junction Improvement, Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The Football (Offences) Act 1991 (Commencement) Order 1991
The Football (Offences) (Designation of Football Matches) Order 1991 (revoked)
The Companies Act 1989 (Register of Auditors and Information about Audit Firms) Regulations 1991
items.description: Regulation 2 of the regulations obliges supervisory bodies recognised under Part II of the Companies Act 1989 (eligibility for appointment as company auditor) to keep a register containing the information specified in the regulation. Regulation 3 of the regulations imposes certain obligations on recognised supervisory bodies in connection with the keeping of the register. Regulation 4 of the regulations obliges each recognised supervisory body to make the information specified in the regulation available to the public. Regulations 5 to 8 of the regulations make provision as to the keeping of the register; as to the inspection of it or of information kept by virtue of regulation 4 and as to the obtaining of copies of entries on the register or of information kept by virtue of regulation 4. Regulations 7 and 8 authorise the charging of fees for inspection and obtaining copies. The regulations implement article 28 of the Eighth Council Directive of 10 April 1984 on the approval of persons responsible for carrying out the statutory audits of accounting documents (84/253/EEC) (L.126/20).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) Order 1987 (the principal Order). The principal Order specifies classes for the purposes of section 55(2)(f) of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990, which provides that a change of use of a building or other land does not involve development for the purposes of the Act if the new use and the former use are both within the same specified class.

items.title: The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 9) Order 1991
The Building Standards (Relaxation by Local Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with amendments tha Building Standards (Relaxation by Local Authorities) (Scotland) Regulations 1985. The Regulations come into force on 1st April 1991, to coincide with the coming into force of the Building Standards (Scotland) Regulations 1990.

items.title: The A38 Trunk Road (Improvement at Twowatersfoot) Order 1991
The Education (Pupils' Attendance Records) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the recording and publication of information about the attendance of registered pupils by amendment of the Pupils' Registration Regulations 1956 (“the 1956 Regulations”), the Education (Schools Information) Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”) and the Education (School Curriculum and Related Information) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”), and by new Regulations made under sections 22 and 103 of the Education Reform Act 1988.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 7) Order 1991
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989.

items.title: The Access to Personal Files (Social Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Human Fertilisation and Embryology (Special Exemptions) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for two exceptions to the general rule that any person keeping or using human embryos, or storing human gametes, may do so only in accordance with a licence from the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Authority (“the Authority”).

items.title: The Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. The principal changes are as follows:

items.title: The Crop Residues (Restrictions on Burning) (No. 2) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, impose restrictions on burning of certain crop residues on agricultural land (regulation 3). The crop residues to which the Regulations apply (Schedule 1) are:—

items.title: The Hormonal Substances (Food Sources) (Animals) Regulations 1991
The Leeds General Infirmary and Associated Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1991
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS Trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Leeds General Infirmary and Associated Hospitals National Health Service Trust to the United Leeds Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust and makes consequential amendments to the Order.

items.title: The Child Benefit and Social Security (Fixing and Adjustment of Rates) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Child Benefitand Social Security (Fixing and Adjustment of Rates) Regulations 1976 by providing that the weekly rate of child benefit in respect of an only, elder or eldest child shall be increased to £9.25and in respect of other children to £7.50.

items.title: The Essex Water Company (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1991
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Bathing Waters (Classification) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a system of classifying the quality of relevant territorial waters, coastal waters and inland waters which are bathing waters. The classification BW1 prescribed by the Regulations reflects the mandatory standards laid down in the Annex to Council directive 76/160/EEC concerning the quality of bathing water.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Community Charge Benefits (Miscellaneous) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Community Charge Benefits (General) Regulations 1989 so that with respect to each benefit–

items.title: The Building (Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
The Income Support (Transitional) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (Transitional) Regulations 1987 to provide that a person’s transitional addition or special transitional addition in respect of income support shall not be reduced where there has been an increase in his income attributable to the receipt of an increase of child benefit having effect from the week beginning 7th October 1991, or an increase in his applicable amount in consequence of the child benefit increase.

items.title: The Fodder Plant Seeds (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: (This is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No.5) Order 1991
The Bathing Waters (Classification) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, prescribe a system of classification for determining the quality of relevant territorial waters, coastal waters and inland waters which are bathing waters. The classification BW1 prescribed by the Regulations reflects the mandatory standards laid down in the Annex to Council Directive 76/160/EEC (O.J. No. L31, 5.2.76, p.1) concerning the quality of bathing water.

items.title: The Retirement Benefits Schemes (Restriction on Discretion to Approve) (Small Self-administered Schemes) Regulations 1991
items.description: Section 591(6) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 provides that regulations made by the Board for the purposes of that section may restrict the Board’s discretion to approve a retirement benefits scheme “a scheme” by reference to the benefits provided by the scheme, the investments held for the purposes of the scheme, the manner in which the scheme is administered or any other circumstances whatever; and these Regulations, which come into force on 5th August 1991, impose restrictions in relation to small self-administered schemes.

items.title: The County Council of Somerset (Bridgwater Bypass) (North) (River Parrett Bridge) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1991
The Dartmoor National Park (Restriction of Agricultural Operations) Order 1991
items.description: This Order applies section 42(2) of the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (which imposes restrictions on agricultural operations) to an area, shown on the map referred to in this Order, of approximately 250 acres of moor or heath at Cator Common, Cator, Widecombe, in the county of Devon within the Dartmoor National Park.

items.title: The Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations 1979 by providing that there shall be no adjustment of benefit payable under the Social Security Act 1975 in respect of any increase in the weekly rate of child benefit arising as a consequence of the Child Benefit and Social Security (Fixing and Adjustment of Rates) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Control of Pollution (Amendment) Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings the Control of Pollution (Amendment) Act 1989 into force in three stages–

items.title: The Isle of Wight Light Railway Order 1991
The M5 Birmingham-Exeter Motorway (Strensham Re-Located Northbound Service Area) Connecting Roads Scheme 1991
The Construction Products Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 89/106/EEC on the approximation of laws, regulations and administrative provisions of the member States relating to construction products “the Directive”. The Directive is based on the Council resolution of 7th May 1985 on a new approach to technical harmonisation and standards (OJ No. C136, 4.6.85, p. 1).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.7) (Patents Rules) 1991
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session to make new rules for causes relating to Patents, Designs, Copyright and Trade Marks. In particular it introduces, in relation to causes relating to patents, a pre-proof hearing at which the court will consider the future progress of the case. It also repeals section 103(2) of the Patents Act 1949 so that (a) an application to the Court of Session for revocation of a patent under the Act of 1949 or under the Patents Act 1977 shall in either case be by petition and (b) the current rules for service shall apply to such a petition.

items.title: The Controlled Waste (Registration of Carriers and Seizure of Vehicles) Regulations 1991
items.description: The Control of Pollution (Amendment) Act 1989 makes it a criminal offence for a person who is not a registered carrier to transport controlled waste to or from any place in Great Britain. lt also provides for the seizure and disposal of vehicles used for illegal waste disposal.

items.title: The Recovery of Tax in Summary Proceedings (Financial Limits) Order 1991
The Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) (Amendment) Rules 1991
The Patents (Fees) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Patents (Fees) (No. 2) Rules 1990 (S.I. 1990/2517). As the result of the rationalisation of the general fee structure, fees in respect of several matters, particularly, under the Patents Act 1949 (Part B of the Schedule) have been abolished. However, fees in respect of other matters have been increased. The increases take effect from 12 August 1991, except for the fees taken in relation to international applications (rule 4 and Part C of the Schedule) which take effect from 9th September 1991.

items.title: The Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Oxford Cable Limited) Order 1991
The Farm and Conservation Grant Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations implement provisions in Title 1 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p. 1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures (as amended by Council Regulations (EEC) Nos. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p. 1), 1137/88 (OJ No. L108, 29.4.88, p.1), 2156/89 (OJ No. L207, 19.7.89, p. 12) and 3808/89 (OJ No. L371,20.12.89, p. 1)), and Article 1 of Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (OJ No. L128, 19.5.75, p. 1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85.

items.title: The Farm Woodland (Variation) Scheme 1991
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No.4) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”) to allow employers to pay earnings-related contributions to the Inland Revenue quarterly in certain circumstances, instead of monthly. The circumstances are where the employer has reasonable grounds for believing that, in the current income tax year, his average monthly payment to the Collector of Taxes will be less than £400 in the total amount in respect of–

items.title: The Broads Authority (Pilotage Powers) Order 1991
The Representation of the People Act 1991 (Commencement) Order 1991
The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (England and Wales) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (England and Wales) Order 1988. It prescribes increased fees in respect of export certification services and import licensing services (article 2).

items.title: The North Hull Housing Action Trust (Area and Constitution) Order 1991
The Human Organ Transplants and the United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1991
The Companies (Disclosure of Interests in Shares) (Orders imposing restrictions on shares) Regulations 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Mercury Personal Communications Network Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Mercury Personal Communications Network Limited a licence on 9th July 1991 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 22nd July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Mercury Personal Communications Network Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 21st August 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Microtel Communications Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Microtel Communications Limited a licence on 9th July 1991 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 22nd July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Microtel Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 21st August 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Unitel Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Unitel Limited a licence on 9th July 1991 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 22nd July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Unitel Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 21st August 1991.

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment (No. 5) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 by providing that, where a person is in residential accommodation provided or arranged by the local authority, he shall continue to be treated as in the same category of residential accommodation for the purposes of determining his applicable amount notwithstanding the fact that the home is or becomes a residential care home.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Exceptions) (Wales) Regulations 1991
The Education (School Curriculum and Related Information) (Wales) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations impose requirements on local education authorities, governing bodies and headteachers relating to the provision of information as to schools and admission arrangements in relation to school years. These Regulations which apply only to schools in Wales revoke the Education (School Information) Regulations 1981, as amended (regulation 1(4)) in so far as they apply to Wales and substantively re-enact them (regulations 2 to 7 and 12 to 14 and Schedules 1 and 2) together with provision relating to requirements to provide information about the curriculum in schools (regulations 8 to 11).

items.title: The Contracting-Out (Protection of Pensions) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, Regulations relating to the protection of pensions provisions (sometimes called the “anti-franking” provisions) of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975.

items.title: The Treasury Bills (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in History) (Wales) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying appropriate targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Preservation of Benefit) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, Regulations relating to the preservation of benefits under occupational pension schemes.

items.title: The Civil Aviation Authority Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations consolidate and amend the Civil Aviation Authority Regulations 1983, as amended. In addition to some minor and drafting amendments, the following changes have been made—

items.title: The Police (Discipline) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Representation of the People (Northern Ireland) (Variation of Specified Documents and Amendment) Regulations 1991
The European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Slaughter of Poultry (Licences and Specified Qualifications) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to the slaughter of poultry, provide for the licensing of poultry slaughtermen and specify qualifications which must be held by persons supervising the premises used in connection with the slaughter of poultry.

items.title: The Children (Allocation of Proceedings) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the allocation of certain proceedings concerning children between the High Court, the county courts and the magistrates' courts.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (High Security Prisons) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (High Security Prisons) Regulations 1989 by imposing restrictions on helicopters flying in specified airspace around the high security prison at Whitemoor.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Revaluation) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, Regulations relating to the revaluation of pensions under occupational pension schemes.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No.6) Order 1991
The Testing in Primary Schools (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Testing in Primary Schools (Scotland) Regulations 1990 in three respects. Firstly, they alter the latest date by which an education authority are required to order attainment test materials in respect of a school year from 15th November to 31st March, the amendment having effect from 19th August 1991. Secondly, they remove the requirement that attainment tests which are to be held in each school year are to be held within the period 1st March to 30th April. In respect of the school year commencing on 1st August 1991, tests may be held at any time during that school year from 19th August 1991, being the date these Regulations come into force. In respect of future school years, tests may be held at any time during the school year. Thirdly, they restrict the requirement to ensure that a summary of test results in a school is provided to the school board of that school so that it applies only where it is possible to give such summary without identifying an individual pupil’s results.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Welsh) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: Section 10(3) of the Education Reform Act 1988 imposes on local education authorities, governing bodies and head teachers of schools the duty to teach the subjects comprising the National Curriculum including Welsh for a reasonable time during each of the key stages defined in the Act. This duty is replaced by the provisions of the attainment targets and programmes of study in a subject in the National Curriculum when these come into force by order of the Secretary of State. In respect of Welsh, the attainment targets and programmes of study come into force over a period of time by virtue of the provisions of the Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Welsh) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/1082) “the principal Order”.

items.title: The Deposit Protection Board (Increase of Borrowing Limit) Order 1991
The House of Commons Members' Fund (Variation) Order 1991
The Representation of the People Act 1990 (Commencement No.2) Order 1991
The Returning Officers' Charges Order 1991
The Returning Officer’s Charges (Northern Ireland) Order 1991
items.description: This Order specifies, in relation to Northern Ireland, the expenses incurred by the returning officer for the purposes of or in connection with a parliamentary election in respect of which he is entitled to recover his charges. The Chief Electoral Officer for Northern Ireland is the returning officer for each constituency in Northern Ireland by virtue of section 26 of the Representation of the People Act 1983. The Schedule to the Order is in two parts–

items.title: Child Minding and Day Care (Applications for Registration) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations require that child minders and providers of day care provide the information specified in Schedules 1 and 2 when applying for registration under the Children Act 1989.

items.title: The Price Marking (Amendment) Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (General Cable Limited)(Bradford) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to General Cable Limited on 19th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the City of Bradford area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 29th July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, General Cable Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its City of Bradford public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 26th August 1991.

items.title: The A11 Trunk Road (Besthorpe to Wymondham Improvement) Detrunking Order 1991
The A11 Trunk Road (Besthorpe to Wymondham Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1991
The International Organisations (Miscellaneous Exemptions) Order 1991
The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations 1991
The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Order 1991
The Air Navigation (Overseas Territories) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order further amends the Air Navigation (Overseas Territories) Order 1989 as amended by the Air Navigation (Overseas Territories) (Amendment) Order 1991. In addition to minor and drafting amendments the following changes are made—

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No.2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1986. It adds New Zealand and Mexico to the list of the Contracting States to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction signed at The Hague on 25th October 1980 (Cm. 33).

items.title: The Extradition (Aviation Security) Order 1991
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences under sections 2 and 3 of the Aviation Security Act 1982 (c. 36) (damaging or endangering the safety of aircraft) and section 1 of the Aviation and Maritime Security Act 1990 (endangering safety at aerodromes), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States Parties to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Civil Aviation, signed at Montreal on 23rd September 1971 and to the Protocol for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts of Violence at Airports Serving International Civil Aviation, signed at Montreal on 24th February 1988; it does not apply to those States Parties (Commonwealth countries and Parties to the European Convention on Extradition) in relation to which the definition of extraditable offence already includes offences of the kind mentioned in this Order. The Order revokes and replaces the Extradition (Protection of Aircraft) Order 1973 and amendments to that Order.

items.title: The Extradition (Designated Commonwealth Countries) Order 1991
items.description: This Order designates certain Commonwealth countries for the time being mentioned in Schedule 3 to the British Nationality Act 1981 (c. 61) for the purposes of the Extradition Act 1989.

items.title: The Extradition (Drug Trafficking) Order 1991
items.description: This Order applies the Extradiction Act 1989 so as to make extraditable offences within the meaning of the Drug Trafficking Offences Act 1986 (c. 32) and offences to which section 1 of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 (c. 41) relates, attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to certain States, Parties to the Convention against the Illicit Traffic in Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances, signed at Vienna on 20th December 1988; it does not apply to those States Parties (Commonwealth countries and Parties to the European Convention on Extradition) in relation to which the definition of extraditable offence already includes offences of the kind mentioned in the Order.

items.title: The Extradition (Torture) Order 1991
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable the offence described in section 134 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (c. 33) and an attempt to commit such an offence in the case of States Parties to the Convention Against Torture and other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations on 10th December 1984; it does not apply to those States Parties (Commonwealth countries and Parties to the European Convention on Extradition) in relation to which the definition of extraditable offence already includes offences of the kind mentioned in the Order, except to the extent that extradition arrangements continue in force in respect of extradition between Parties to the European Convention and one or more of the territories specified in Schedule 5.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Bermuda) Order 1991
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with minor and drafting amendments the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Bermuda) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1334), which extended to Bermuda certain provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988, subject to the modifications specified in Schedules 1 and 2. The 1989 Order had not been brought into force in Bermuda pursuant to the provisions of Article 7 thereof.

items.title: The Vienna Document (Privileges and Immunities) Order 1991
The Dentists Act 1984 (Dental Auxiliaries) Amendment Order 1991
The Dental Auxiliaries (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Broadcasting Act 1990 (Guernsey) (No. 2) Order 1991
The Broadcasting Act 1990 (Jersey) (No. 2) Order 1991
The Local Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order makes miscellaneous amendments to the law relating to local elections in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (Commencement) Order 1991
The Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (Guernsey) Order 1991
The Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (Jersey) Order 1991
The Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (Isle of Man) Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (British Cable Services Limited) (Cardiff) Order 1991
The Extradition (Protection of Nuclear Material) Order 1991
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Act 1989 so as to make extraditable the offences described in the Nuclear Material (Offences) Act 1983 (c.18), attempts to commit such offences and participation in the commission of such offences. It applies to States Parties to the Convention on the Physical Protection of Nuclear Material, opened for signature at New York and Vienna on 3rd March 1980; it does not apply to those States Parties (Commonwealth countries and Parties to the European Convention on Extradition) in relation to which the definition of extraditable offence already includes offences of the kind mentioned in this Order.

items.title: The Territorial Sea Act 1987 (Isle of Man) Order 1991
items.description: This Order extends the Territorial Sea Act to the 1987 to the Isle of Man with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Family Law Act 1986 (Dependent Territories) Order 1991
items.description: Part I of the Family Law Act 1986 sets up a scheme of jurisdiction of courts in the United Kingdom to make orders with regard to the custody of children and provides for the recognition and enforcement of such orders throughout the United Kingdom. This Order modifies Part I so as to bring orders made by courts in dependent territories within the scheme and to provide for the recognition and enforcement of such orders in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Reciprocal Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Canada) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Films Co-Production Agreements (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: The Films Co-Production Agreements Order 1985 (“the principal Order”) provides that films made in accordance with the agreements between the Government of the United Kingdom and the Governments of the countries listed in the Schedule, as amended by S.I. 1985/2001, 1990/1513, are to be treated as British films for the purposes of Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985 (Certification for the purposes of section 72 of the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39) in case of British films). The present Order extends the list of agreements scheduled to the principal Order to include a further amendment made by a Protocol dated 5th July 1991 (Cm. 1592) to the agreement with Canada of 12th September 1975 (Cmnd. 6380) as amended on 9th July 1985 (Cmnd. 9887).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Second Amendment) Order 1991
The Motor Vehicles (International Circulation) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1991
The Transfer of Functions (Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1991
items.description: This Order transfers to the Secretary of State the functions of the Treasury under sections 29 and 47(1) of the Representation of the People Act 1983 ( 1983 Act as amended by the Representation of the People Act 1991. The functions transferred relate to returning officers' services and expenses in respect of parliamentary elections, and the loan of equipment for local elections. Various consequential and supplementary amendments are made to the 1983 Act and to the Representation of the People Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1081) and the Representation of the People (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1111). There is a saving for charges in respect of elections which have already taken place before the coming into force of the Order.

items.title: The Building Societies Act 1986 (Modifications) Order 1991 (revoked)
items.description: This Order is made under section 104 of the Building Societies Act 1986 for the purpose of assimilating in two respects the law relating to building societies to that relating to companies in Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (United Artists Communications (South Thames Estuary) Limited) Order 1991
The Local Government Finance (Repeals and Consequential Amendments) Order 1991
The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Hearing Aid Council (Disciplinary Proceedings) Legal Assessor (Amendment) Rules 1991
The Mid Southern Water Company (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1991
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they are approved by the Secretary of State and come into force on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Northampton Cable Television Limited) (Northeast Northamptonshire) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Northampton Cable Television Limited on 27th March 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Boroughs of Kettering and Wellingborough and the Districts of Corby, East Northamptonshire and Harborough. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament onr 29th July 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Northampton Cable Television Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Northeast Northamptonshire public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 26th August 1991.

items.title: The Agricultural Marketing Act 1958 and Milk Marketing Schemes (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 22nd August 1991 and further amend the Agricultural Marketing Act 1958 and the 3 Milk Marketing Schemes that apply to Scotland.

items.title: The United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority (Limit on Borrowing) Order 1991
items.description: Section 3(1) of the Atomic Energy Authority Act 1986 limits the aggregate amount outstanding by way of principal in respect of borrowing and guarantees in connection with the finances of the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority.

The Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation Order 1991
The Free Zone (Prestwick Airport) Designation Order 1991
The Free Zone (Southampton) Designation Order 1991
The Finance Act 1991 (Savings-related Share Option Schemes) (Appointed Day) Order 1991
The Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 (Commencement and Appointed Day) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th August 1991 the provisions of the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 except section 8 thereof (Power to make corresponding provision for Northern Ireland) which came into force on Royal Assent. The Order also makes 30th November 1991 the appointed day for the purposes of section 1of the Act that is the day after which, subject to exceptions, possession or custody of a dog to which section 1 applies is prohibited.

items.title: The Dangerous Dogs (Designated Types) Order 1991
The Dangerous Dogs Compensation and Exemption Schemes Order 1991
items.description: Section 1(3) of the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 (the Act) provides that possession or custody of dogs to which section 1 of the Act applies will be prohibited after a day to be appointed. By virtue of the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 (Commencement and Appointed Day) Order 1991 (S.I. 1991/1742), that day is 30th November 1991.

items.title: The Montrose Harbour Revision Order 1991
items.description: This Order–

The Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Act 1975 (“the Act”) and the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”).
