The British Technology Group Act 1991 (Nominated Company) Order 1991
The A428 Trunk Road (Bedford Southern Bypass) Order 1991
The Customs Controls on Importation of Goods Regulations 1991 (revoked)
items.description: From 1st January 1992 the customs procedure to be applied on the arrival of goods from places outside the EC will be governed by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4151/88 . From that date wharves, transit sheds and airport examination stations for the loading, unloading and storage of goods imported from outside the EC will be approved by the Commissioners directly under that regulation.

items.title: The Customs Warehousing Regulations 1991
items.description: From 1st January 1992 the approval and administration of customs warehouses will be governed by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2503/88 and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 2561/90, except in the case of victualling warehouses which will continue to be approved under section 92(2) of the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979 for the purposes of Article 38 of Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3665/87.

items.title: The Customs Warehousing (Victualling) Regulations 1991
items.description: Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3665/87, which lays down rules for the application of the system of Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) export refunds, provides that supplies which are intended for the victualling of (ie for use as stores on) seagoing vessels, aircraft serving international (including intra-Community) routes and oil rigs, are treated as exports from the Community. A victualling warehouse is a customs warehouse specifically approved for the advance payment of CAP export refunds to certain exporters of agricultural produce. Eligible goods qualify for the advance payment of export refund at the time of their deposit in a victualling warehouse.

items.title: The Free Zone Regulations 1991
items.description: From 1st January 1992 the administration of free zones will be governed by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2504/88 and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 2562/90. The provisions of the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979 which are superseded by that legislation are repealed by these Regulations.

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 4 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Planning and Compensation Act 1991.

items.title: The Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 8 Regulations 1991
The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1991
The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 (Commencement No.7) Order 1991
The Judicial Pensions (Widowers' and Children’s Benefits) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the contributions to be made by women who are holders of certain judicial offices towards pensions for widowers and children under Part II of the Judicial Pensions Act 1981. That Act formerly provided for the payment of pensions to widows of men who were holders of those judicial offices, but there was no provision for widowers' pensions, which were first introduced by the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990. These Regulations have similar effect to the Judicial Pensions (Widows' and Childrens' Benefits) Regulations 1987, which continue to apply to the contributions made by male office-holders.

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) Regulations 1991
The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Persons) Order 1991
The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Naval Courts-Martial General Orders (Royal Navy) 1991
items.description: These General Orders prescribe rules for the procedure to be followed, and the rules of evidence in naval courts-martial. The rules of procedure are in general accord with procedures in the criminal courts in England. Special provision is made for trials in which navigation is an issue. These Orders replace and revoke Orders made in 1957 and take account of changes in the law and procedure since then. Such changes include the removal of the right for the accused to make an unsworn statement from his or her place, ensuring that prospective members of the court-martial do not know the nature of the charges in advance of the trial and introduction of revised rules for adducing expert evidence.

items.title: The Building Societies Act 1986 (Modifications) (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order is made under section 104 of the Building Societies Act 1986 for the purposes of assimilating in respect of directors and auditors the law relating to building societies to that relating to companies in Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1989 as follows:

The Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations replace with amendments the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) (No. 2) Regulations 1975 (S.I. 1975/598) and are consequential upon the changes made to attendance allowance by section 2(1) of the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 which provides in particular that a person shall not be entitled to an attendance allowance unless he is aged 65 or over.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1968) (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”) and are consequential upon the coming into force of the Disability Living Allowanceand Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 which makes provision for disability living allowanceand disability working allowanceand revokes section 37A of the Social Security Act 1975 under which mobility allowance is paid.

items.title: The Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance (Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make consequential amendments to the following Regulationsand Orders as a result of the introduction of disability living allowance and disability working allowance by inserting references to disability living allowance and disability working allowance–

The Electrically, Hydraulically and Oil-Electrically Operated Lifts (Components) (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1991
items.description: The Regulations supersede the Electrically Operated Lifts (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1500) which are referred to in this note as the 1986 Regulations.

items.title: The Simple Pressure Vessels (Safety) Regulations 1991 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 87/404/EEC on the harmonisation of the laws of the Member States relating to simple pressure vessels as amended by Council Directive 90/488/EEC.

items.title: The Community Health Councils (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: Regulation 2(5) of the Community Health Councils Regulations 1985 requires a District Health Authority in Wales, in accordance with any directions which may be given by the Secretary of State, to assist the Secretary of State in—

The Haddock (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1991
The Building Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations made under the Building Act 1984 revoke and replace with amendments the Building Regulations 1985, and consolidate all subsequent amendments to those Regulations. They impose requirements on people carrying out certain building operations.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Avon) Order 1991
The Building Societies (General Charge and Fees) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Building Societies (General Charge and Fees) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”), which are revoked. They provide for a general charge to be paid by authorised building societies towards the expenses of the Building Societies Commission. The charge is levied with respect to the Commission’s accounting year beginning on 1st April 1991 and ending on 31st March 1992 and is expected to raise £3.2 million. Each society is required to pay a sum of £1,100 plus a sum equal to 0.00162% of its assets, up to £20,000 million, and 0.00081% of its assets above that amount, or in the case of a Society formed by a recent amalgamation, a sum of £2,00O plus a sum equal to the same percentage of the aggregated value of the uniting societies.

items.title: The Hearing Aid Council Investigating and Disciplinary Committee Rules Approval Instrument 1991
items.description: This Instrument contains the rules, approved by the Lord Chancellor, which are to govern the constitution of the Investigating Committee and the constitution and procedure of the Disciplinary Committee set up by the Hearing Aid Council in accordance with the provisions of the Hearing Aid Council Act 1968. The Instrument replaces, with effect from 1st January 1992, the Hearing Aid Council Disciplinary Committee (Procedure) Rules Approval Instrument 1971.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (Movers Lane Flyover, Barking and Dagenham) (Weight Restriction) Order 1991
The Social Security (Credits) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Waltham Forest Housing Action Trust (Area and Constitution) Order 1991
The Personal Equity Plan (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 1992, further amend the Personal Equity Plan Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”). The amendments have two main effects. The first is to provide for an additional kind of plan, called a single company plan, to which a plan investor can subscribe in any year in addition to subscribing to a plan under the Regulations as they were before these amendments, which is now called a general plan. The subscription limit for a single company plan is half that for a general plan and its distinguishing features are that only one company is designated for the purposes of the plan by the plan manager and that, in general, only shares in the designated company may be held under the plan.

items.title: The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1991
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Radioactivity in Sheep) (England) (Partial Revocation) Order 1991
The Education (London Residuary Body) (Property Transfer) (No. 4) Order 1991
items.description: This Order makes provision for the transfer to specified London borough councils of certain property, rights and liabilities originally transferred to, or held, acquired or incurred by the London Residuary Body (“the LRB”) by virtue of, or in the exercise of any of its functions under, Part III of the Education Reform Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”).

items.title: The Provision of Confidential Statistical Informationto the Statistical Office of the European Communities (Restriction on Disclosure) Regulations 1991 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement article 6 of Council Regulation (EURATOM, EEC) No. 1588/90 of 11th June 1990 on the transmission of data subject to statistical confidentiality to the Statistical Office of the European Communities (“the SOEC”).

items.title: The Valuation for Rating (Former Enterprise Zones) Regulations 1991
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Radioactivity in Sheep) (Wales) (Partial Revocation) Order 1991
The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, charging authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Provisional amounts are paid during the year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the detailed rules for the calculation of payments contained in the Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Wales) Regulations 1989. The amendments include changes to the provision as regards the costs of collection and recovery and the distribution of the allowance between authorities (regulation 2(a)(ii)). They also include the substitution of amounts prescribed for the calculation of provisional amounts and relating to enterprise zones and repayments and interest (regulation 2(c) and (d)).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1991
The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (England) (No. 3) Order 1991
The Civil Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1991
The Rules of Procedure (Air Force) (Amendment) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules further amend the Rules of Procedure (Air Force) 1972, consequent upon the passing of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (c. 33) (the 1988 Act) and the Armed Forces Act 1991 (c. 62) (the 1991 Act). The amendments are contained in the Schedule to the Rules.

items.title: The Rules of Procedure (Army) (Amendment) Rules 1991
items.description: These Rules further amend the Rules of Procedure (Army) 1972, consequent upon the passing of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (c. 33) (the 1988 Act) and the Armed Forces Act 1991 (c. 62) (the 1991 Act). The amendments are contained in the Schedule to the Rules.

items.title: The Standing Civilian Courts (Areas) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Standing Civilian Courts (Areas) Order 1977 (S.I. 1977/89) by removing Berlin from the areas specified as those for which trials may be directed to be held before Standing Civilian Courts of civilians subject to the Army Act 1955 (c. 18) or the Air Force Act 1955 (c. 19), for offences committed in that area or elsewhere.

items.title: The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (A1 Great North Way/Falloden Way, A598 Regents Park Road/Finchley Road Junctions Improvements) Trunk Roads and Slip Roads Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cablevision Communications Company of Hertfordshire Limited) (East Hertfordshire) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cablevision Communications Company of Hertfordshire Limited a licence on 11th January 1991 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in east Hertfordshire. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 25th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cablevision Communications Company of Hertfordshire Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its East Hertfordshire public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 25th March 1991.

items.title: The Private Water Supplies Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations supplement Chapter III (Quality and Sufficiency of Supplies) of Part III of the Water Industry Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). They are concerned with the quality of water supplied from private supplies in England and Wales for drinking, washing or cooking or for food production purposes. The Regulations come into force on 1st January 1992.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment)(No. 5) Regulations 1991
The Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) (Third Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, charging authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Provisional amounts are paid during the year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. Regulation 2 of these Regulations further amends the detailed rules for the calculation of payments contained in the Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) Regulations 1989. The amendments include changes to the allowance for the costs of collection and recovery (regulation 2(b)(ii)), and the distribution of the allowance between authorities (regulation 2(c)). They also include the substitution of amounts prescribed for the calculation of provisional amounts relating to enterprise zones and repayments and interest (both in regulation 2(e)).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Development Plan) Regulations 1991
items.description: These regulations make provision in respect of the form and content of structure plans, local plans, minerals local plans, waste local plans and unitary development plans made under the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 and in respect of the procedure to be followed in connection with the making, alteration and replacement of such plans. They also set out rules for resolving conflict between and within such plans and contain transitional provisions to cover the changeover to the development plan system under the 1990 Act as amended by the Planning and Compensation Act 1991.

items.title: The Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964 (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order redefines the activities in relation to which the Construction Industry Training Board (the Board) exercises its functions. The Board was established on 21st July 1964 by the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964 (the 1964 Order). Its activities were redefined by the Industrial Training (Construction Board) Order 1964 (Amendment) Order 1990 (the 1990 Order).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Enforcement Notices and Appeals) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with amendments the Town and Country Planning (Enforcement Notices and Appeals) Regulations 1981.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988. The main changes are—

The Personal Community Charge (Reduction Scheme) (England) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the reduction in certain cases of the amount that a person is liable to pay by way of personal community charge. They have effect in relation to personal community charges for the financial year beginning 1st April 1992.

items.title: The Income Tax (Purchased Life Annuities) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st March 1992, are made under sections 656(2)(a) and (4)(c) and 658(3) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Taxes Act”), and make further amendments to the Income Tax (Purchased Life Annuities) Regulations 1956 (S.I. 1956/1230) (“the principal Regulations”).

It provides for the extension of the definition of disease in the Animal Health Act 1981 to include anthrax and the definitions of animal and poultry to include any kind of mammal except man, and quail respectively (article 3).

items.title: The Domestic Property (Valuation) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 2 (basis of valuation) of the Domestic Property (Valuation)Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Marriage Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1991

items.description: This version of the The Fertilisers (Sampling and Analysis) (Amendment) Regulations 1991 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Premises (Registration) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for the registration of food premises (including vehicles and other moveable structures) by food authorities.

Scottish Enterprise and Highlands and Islands Enterprise (Transfer of Property, Rights and Liabilities) Order 1991
items.description: This Order transfers from the Secretary of State to Scottish Enterprise and Highlands and Islands Enterprise all property, rights and liabilities of the part of his Department known immediately before the coming into force of this Order as the Training Agency in Scotland to which the Secretary of State is entitled or subject and which in his opinion are connected with the carrying out by him in Scotland of functions conferred by or under the Employment and Training Act 1973.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1991
The Registered Foreign Lawyers Order 1991
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Experimental Traffic (Amendment) (No.2) Order 1991
The Environmental Protection (Duty of Care) Regulations 1991 (revoked)
items.description: Section 34(1) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 imposes a duty of care on any person who imports, produces, carries, keeps, treats or disposes of controlled waste or, as a broker, has control of such waste. The duty requires such persons to ensure that there is no unauthorised or harmful deposit, treatment or disposal of the waste, to prevent the escape of the waste from their control or that of any other person, and on the transfer of the waste to ensure that the transfer is only to an authorised person or to a person for authorised transport purposes and that a written description of the waste is also transferred.

items.title: The Hereford and Worcester and Shropshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
The Feeding Stuffs Regulations 1991
The Animals, Meat and Meat Products (Examination for Residues and Maximum limits) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact provisions formerly contained in the Animals and Fresh Meat (Examination for Residues) Regulations 1988 (in so far as they related to Great Britain), the Meat and Meat Products (Hormonal Substances) Regulations 1989, the Meat and Meat Products (Hormonal Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 and the Hormonal Substances (Food Sources) (Animals) Regulations 1991 concerning the examination of animals, meat and meat products for the presence of residues and taking of further action in the event of any animal, or meat product being found to contain a residue of a hormonal substance.  In addition, the Regulations contain various new provisions prohibiting the presence in food and food sources of unlicensed substances and regulating the presence in food and food sources of authorised substances by specifying maximum residue limits which will apply in respect of such substances.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1991
The Education (School Government) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Mackerel (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1991
items.description: This Order prohibits (subject to an exception) fishing for mackerel (Scomber scombrus) by handline or rod and line during the period beginning with the date of coming into force of the Order and ending immediately before 1st January 1992—

items.title: The North Yorkshire, South Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of North Yorkshire, South Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire. Consequential changes to districts, parishes and local government electoral areas and to the areas of petty sessional divisions and Coroners' districts are made.

items.title: The British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989 (Activating No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order gives effect to the penalty fares provisions contained in sections 4 to 9 of the British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989, with respect to the train services specified in the Schedule, as from 20th January 1992.

items.title: The A50 and A453 Trunk Roads (Flagstaff Interchange and Link Road) Detrunking Order 1991
The Dorset and Somerset (County Boundaries) Order 1991
The Finance Act 1990, section 46, (Appointed Day) Order 1991
The Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 (Commencement No. 9) Order 1991
items.description: This Order appoints 31st December 1991 as the date for the coming into force of the provisions of the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 which are specified in the Schedule to the Order insofar as these are not already in force. The provisions so specified contain amendments to the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 (c. 66).

items.title: The Saithe (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1991
items.description: This Order prohibits, subject to an exception, fishing for saithe (Pollachius virens) during the period beginning with the date of coming into force of the Order and ending immediately before 1st January 1992—

items.title: The Broadcasting (Programme Contractors' Additional Payments) Order 1991
The Police (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1991
The Durham and North Yorkshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No. 4) Order 1991
The Virgin Islands (Constitution) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends section 19 of the Virgin Islands (Constitution) Order 1976 by giving the Governor certain powers to delegate to theChief Minister and other Ministers responsibility for matters relating to external affairs or internal security, as well as giving the Governor power to give directions with regard to matters falling within the Governor’s special responsibilities.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Designated Countries and Territories) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides that, subject to certain modifications, Part VI of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 applies to an order made by a court of a designated country or territory for the purposes of recovering property obtained as a result of or in connection with conduct corresponding to an offence to which Part VI of the Act applies, recovering the value of property so obtained or depriving a person of a pecuniary advantage so obtained. Part VI of the Act as modified will also apply to proceedings which have been or are to be instituted in a designated country or territory and may result in such an order being made there. Offences to which Part VI of the Act apply are indictable offences (other than drug trafficking offences and offences under Part III of the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989) and certain exceptional summary maxima offences.

items.title: The Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance (Northern Ireland Consequential Amendments) Order 1991
items.description: This Order makes amendments to certain Acts which apply either in Northern Ireland only or in the whole of the United Kingdom, being amendments consequential on the Disability Living Allowanceand Disability Working Allowance (Northern Ireland) Order 1991.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Guernsey) Order 1991
items.description: This Order extends the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 specified in Part I of the Schedule to the Order to the Bailiwick of Guernsey subject to the modifications specified in Part II of that Schedule.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Czechoslovakia) Order 1991
items.description: The Convention with the Czech and Slovak Federal Republic is set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Denmark) Order 1991
items.description: The Protocol scheduled to this Order makes certain alterations to the Convention set out in the Schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Denmark) Order 1980.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Finland) Order 1991
items.description: The Protocol scheduled to this Order makes certain alterations to the Convention set out in the Schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Finland) Order 1970, as amended by three subsequent Protocols.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Iceland) Order 1991
items.description: The Convention with the Republic of Iceland is set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Traffic Areas (Reorganisation) Order 1990
items.description: This Order, in exercise of the powers conferred by sections 3(2) and (3) and 80(2) of the Public Passenger Vehicles Act 1981—

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Isle of Man) Order 1991
items.description: The Arrangement scheduled to this Order makes two amendments to the Arrangement between the United Kingdom and the Isle of Man which is scheduled to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Isle of Man) Order 1955.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Morocco) Order 1991
items.description: Under the Convention with Morocco scheduled to this Order shipping and air transport profits (Article 8), certain trading profits not arising through a permanent establishment (Article 7), non-governmental pensions (Article 18) and the earnings of temporary business visitors (Articles 14 and 15) are, subject to certain conditions, to be taxed only in the country of the taxpayer’s residence. Where an enterprise which is a resident of one country carries on business through a permanent establishment in the other, the profits of the enterprise which are attributable to that permanent establishment may be taxed in that other country (Articles 5 and 7).

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Papua New Guinea) Order 1991
items.description: The Convention with Papua New Guinea is set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1975 (Commencement No.1) Order 1991
The Maximum Number of Judges (Scotland) Order 1991
The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) Order 1983, which enabled regulations to be made giving effect to the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships 1973 and its Protocol of 1978.

items.title: The Ministerial and other Salaries Order 1991
items.description: This Order increases salaries payable under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 to Ministers, to salaried Members of the Opposition, to the Speaker of the House of Commons and to other paid office holders. The amounts specified in this Order are the maxiumum salaries payable. The actual salaries in payment may therefore be below these levels.

items.title: The Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations provide for various matters which affect entitlement to disability working allowance. Regulations 3 and 4 and Schedule 1 concern the disabilities which place the claimant at a disadvantage in getting a job and the declaration of such disability by the claimant. Regulations 5 and 6 prescribe the circumstances in which a person is treated as being in Great Britain and provide for 16 hours' work a week as a minimum requirement. Regulation 7 sets out the qualifying benefits for entitlement to disability working allowance. Regulations 8 to 11 make provision in respect of children and young persons who are normally living with the claimant as members of the household and the circumstances in which a person is to be treated as no longer being a member of the household.

items.title: The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase (No.2) Order 1991
The Social Security (Adjudication) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend provisions in the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/2218) (“the Adjudication Regulations”) and are consequential upon the coming into force of the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991.

items.title: The Fresh Meat Export (Hygiene and Inspection) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fresh Meat Export (Hygiene and Inspection) (Scotland) Regulations 1987 ("the principal Regulations) to take account of certain provisions of Council Directive 88/288/EEC (OJ No. L124,18.5.88, p.28) which further amends Council Directive 64/433/EEC (OJ No. 121, 29.7.64, p.2012/64) on health problems effecting intra-Community trade in fresh meat.

items.title: The Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are consequential upon the introduction of disability living allowance by section 1 of the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (c. 21) (“the 1991 Act”). The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of section 1; the regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24), Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from the requirement under section 10 of the Social Security Act 1980 (c. 30) to refer the proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Social Security (Introduction of Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991
items.description: This instrument contains the first regulations made under section 5 of the Disability Living Allowance and Disability Working Allowance Act 1991 (c. 5). Section 5 enables the Secretary of State to make regulations on matters arising from the substitution of disability living allowance for attendance allowance and mobility allowance and the dissolution of the Attendance Allowance Board. The instrument contains other regulations which are consequential upon the provisions made under section 5.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1991
The A1 Trunk Road (Brownieside De-Trunking) Order 1991
The A1 Trunk Road (Brownieside Improvement) Order 1991
The Domestic Property (Valuation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 2 (basis of valuation) of the Domestic Property (Valuation) (Scotland) Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Mathematics) Order 1991
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Science) Order 1991
items.description: Section 4(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects.

items.title: The South Ayrshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order establishes the South Ayrshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 12A of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by the appropriate health board before its operational date (article 7).

items.title: The Foresterhill Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order establishes the Foresterhill Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 12A of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by the appropriate health board before its operational date (article 7).

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1991
The Milk and Dairies and Milk (Special Designation) (Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No. 13) Order 1991
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) (No.14) Order 1991
The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1991
items.description: Article 3 of this Order brings into force on 2nd January 1992 the following provisions of the Planning and Compensation Act 1991—

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Ports of London and Tilbury) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations are associated with the transfer of property relating to the port of Tilbury from the Port of London Authority to Port of Tilbury London Limited, a company formed by the Authority under section 21 of the Ports Act 1991. The transfer is due to take place before 1st April 1992.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Registered Fever Nurses and Nurses with Ortopaedic and Ophthalmic Qualifications) Amendment Rules Approval Order 1991
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, and made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 (“the principal Rules”) as they relate tothe training of nurses.

items.title: The Tees and Hartlepool Port Authority Scheme 1991 Confirmation Order 1991
items.description: The Order confirms the Tees and Hartlepool Port Authority Scheme 1991, as modified by the Secretary of State for Transport.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order increases the amounts of weekly earnings specified in the secondary earnings brackets in section 4(6E) of the Social Security Act 1975 (“the Act”), in respect of Bracket 1 from £84.99 to £89.99, in respect of Bracket 2 from £85.00 and £129.99 to £90.00 and £134.99, in respect of Bracket 3 from £130.00 and £184.99 to £135.00 and £189.99 and in respect of Bracket 4, from £185.00 to £190.00 (article 2).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1991
The Social Security Benefits Up-rating (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by resolution of each House of Parliament, is made as a consequence of a review under section 63(1) of the Social Security Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”) and includes details of the sums mentioned in that section.

items.title: The Statutory Sick Pay (Rate of Payment) (No. 2) Order 1991
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 6th April 1992, substitutes alternative provisions for the paragraphs of subsection (1) of section 7 of the Social Security and Housing Benefits Act 1982 (rate of payment). The new provisions increase the lower rate of statutory sick pay from £43.50 to £45.30 per week. The higher rate remains unchanged at £52.50 per week. In addition the earnings band is altered so that the higher rate becomes payable where the employee’s earnings are normally £190 or more per week (increased from £185 or more per week) (article 2).

items.title: The Wrexham and East Denbighshire Water Order 1991
The Milford Port Health Authority Order 1991
The Petty Sessional Divisions (Staffordshire) Order 1991
The Combined Probation Areas (Staffordshire) Order 1991
The Combined Probation Areas (Cheshire) Order 1991
The Combined Probation Areas (Cambridgeshire) Order 1991
The Combined Probation Areas (Avon) Order 1991
The Combined Probation Areas (Norfolk) Order 1991
The Teachers (Education, Training and Registration) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
The Nature Conservancy Council (Dissolution) Order 1991
The Non-Domestic Rating (Appropriate Fraction and Rateable Values) Order 1991
The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Quota and other Reliefs) Order 1991
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 1992, provides for reliefs from customs duty (in so far as it is charged) on certain iron and steel products originating in the developing countries named in Schedule 2 to the Order in accordance with a Decision of the Representatives of the Governments of the Member States of the European Coal and Steel Community meeting within the Council, of 3rd December 1991. The reliefs are provided under the Community’s Generalised Tariff Preference Scheme for Developing Countries.

items.title: The Rochdale Healthcare National Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order establishes the Rochdale Healthcare National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Heartland Cablevision (UK) Limited) (Warwick & Stratford-on Avon) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Heartland Cablevision (UK) Limited on 19th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the District of Warwickand part of the District of Stratford-on-Avon. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 27th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Heartland Cablevision (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Warwick and Stratford-on-Avon public telecommunication system when the Order comes into force on 27th March 1991.

items.title: The Imported Food and Feedingstuffs (Safeguards against Cholera) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Tayside Regional Council (Allt Girnaig, Moulin) (Amendment) Water Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Heartland Cablevision II (UK) Limited) (Rugby) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Heartland Cablevision II (UK) Limited on 21st November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in most of the District of Rugby. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 27th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Heartland Cablevision II (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Rugby public telecommunication systems when the Order comes into force on 27th March 1991.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 13) Order 1991
The Education Support Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education Support Grants Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”).

items.title: The Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987 (Continuation) Order 1991
The Companies Act 1989 (Commencement No. 14 and Transitional Provision) Order 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Jones Cable Group of Leeds Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Jones Cable Group of Leeds Limited on 8th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Metropolitan District of Leeds. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 28th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Jones Cable Group of Leeds Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 27th March 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Telecable of Calderdale Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Telecable of Calderdale Limited on 7th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Metropolitan Borough of Calderdale. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 28th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Telecable of Calderdale Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 27th March 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Telecable of Macclesfield Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Telecable of Macclesfield Limited on 19th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in all but the eastermost part of the District of Macclesfield. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 26th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Telecable of Macclesfield Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 25th March 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Telecable of Stockport Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Telecable of Stockport Limited on 8th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the Metropolitan Borough of Stockport. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 26th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Telecable of Stockport Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 25th March 1991.

items.title: The Drivers' Hours (Passenger and Goods Vehicles) (Exemption) (Revocation) Regulations 1991
The Apple Orchard Grubbing Up Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations lay down the measures necessary to supervise and enforce in Great Britain the provisions of Council regulations (EEC) No 1200/90 on the improvement of the Community production of apples and Commission Regulation (EEC) No 2604/90, which contains detailed rules for the applications of the Council Regulation. The Council Regulation and the Commission Regulation provide for the payment of a premium to apple producers who grub up their orchards, and penalize, during the fifteen years following grubbing up, the planting of apple trees by the recipient of the premium on any part of his holding and by subsequent occupiers on the areas grubbed up. The premium is not available to producers of cider apples.

items.title: The Community Drivers' Hours (Passenger and Goods Vehicles)(Temporary Exception) (Revocation) Regulations 1991
The Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations 1976 (“the 1976 Regulations”).

items.title: Offshore Installations (Well Control) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: Regulation 2(2) of the Offshore Installations (Well Control) Regulations 1980 (“the 1980 Regulations”) provides that a driller or drilling supervisor must hold a certificate granted by the Secretary of State on the recommendation of the Petroleum Industry Training Board (“the PITB”) or, until 1st January 1983, one of the certificates specified in the Schedule to those Regulations.

items.title: The Derbyshire, Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire and Warwickshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
The Cholderton (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1991
The Buckinghamshire, Hertfordshire, Northamptonshire and Oxfordshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
The Cheshire, Derbyshire, Hereford and Worcester and Staffordshire (County Boundaries) Order 1991
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of Cheshire, Derbyshire, Hereford and Worcester and Staffordshire. Consequential changes to districts, parishes, local government electoral areas, petty sessional divisions and Coroners' districts are made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (England) Order 1991
items.description: Under Part 1 of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may only be carried out by local authorities if specified conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Public Telecommunications System Designated (Cable Enfield Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Enfield Limited on 25th November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the London Borough of Enfield. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th January 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Enfield Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes inro force on 14th February 1991.

items.title: The Revenue Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1991
The Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations require persons with custody or control of a crop being made into silage, livestock slurry or certain fuel oil to carry out works and take precautions and other steps for preventing pollution of waters which are controlled waters for the purposes of Part III of the Water Act 1989.

items.title: The National Health Service (District Health Authorities) Order 1991
The National Health Service (Determination of Districts) Order 1991
items.description: This Order abolishes on 1st April 1991 the districts of Leeds Eastern Health Authority and Leeds Western Health Authority and merges those districts into a new district called Leeds District. It abolishes the districts of North Warwickshire Health Authority and Rugby Health Authority and merges the districts into a new district called North East Warwickshire District. It abolishes the district of Central Birmingham Health Authority and varies the district of South Birmingham Health Authority to incorporate the area of the former Central Birmingham Health Authority.

items.title: The National Health Service Training Authority (Abolition) Order 1991
The National Health Service Training Authority and Disablement Services Authority Regulations 1991
items.description: The National Health Service Training Authority (Abolition) Order 1991 (S.I. 1991/327) abolishes the National Health Service Training Authority, a special health authority established under the National Health Service Act 1977. These Regulations provide for the transfer of staff from that authority, on its abolition, to the South Western Regional Health Authority (article 2). They also revoke the Regulations which provided for the membership and procedure of and the appointment of officers to that authority (article 3).

items.title: The Regional and District Health Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1991
The Public Telecommunications System Designated (Cable North (Forth District) Limited) (Falkirk and West Lothian) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable North (Forth District) Limited on 11th November 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Falkirk and West Lothian areas. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th January 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable North (Forth District) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its public telecommunications systems in the Falkirk and West Lothian area when Order comes into force on 14th February 1991.

items.title: The Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1991
items.description: Regulation 3 of this Order appoints 1st March 1991 as the date for the coming into force of section 69 (future services to injured person) of the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 (c. 40).

items.title: The High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 1991
The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1991
items.description: This Order increases most of the fees payable to the Principal Clerk of Session and Justiciary, or any officer acting for him, in relation to proceedings in the Court of Session and the specialised courts.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Fees Amendment Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Sheriff Court Fees Order 1985 so as to increase most of the fees payable to the sheriff clerk in relation to proceedings in the sheriff court.

items.title: The Industrial Training (Plastics Processing Board) (Revocation) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides –

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Days in Port) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Sea Fishing (Days in Port) Regulations 1991 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision for the enforcement throughout the United Kingdom of Article 13 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3926/90, fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks, the total allowable catches for 1991 and certain conditions under which they may be fished. The present Regulations make provision for fishing vessels to be exempted from the requirement in the principal Regulations to remain in port for 8 consecutive days in each calendar month (regulation 4). The amendment is consequent upon a decision of the Commission of the European Communities relating to fishing gear. The decision was communicated to the United Kingdom authorities by letter dated 13th February 1991. Copies of the letter are available from the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, Nobel House, 17 Smith Square, London SW1P 3JR, the Scottish Office Agriculture and Fisheries Department, Pentland House, 47 Robb’s Loan, Edinburgh EH14 1TW, the Welsh Office Agriculture Department, Cathays Park, Cardiff CF1 3NQ and the Department of Agriculture for Northern Ireland, Hut 5, Castle Grounds, Stormont, Belfast BT4 3TA.

items.title: The Removal, Storage and Disposal of Vehicles (Prescribed Sums and Charges etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Removal, Storage and Disposal of Vehicles (Prescribed Sums and Charges etc.) Regulations 1989 (as amended by the Removal, Storage and Disposal of Vehicles (Prescribed Sums and Charges etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1990) so as to increase, with effect from 1st April 1991, the sum or charge payable under the 1989 Regulations in respect of the removal by the police or a local authority of a vehicle from £80 to £85 and the daily storage rate under the Regulations in respect of a vehicle whilst it is in the custody of the police or a local authority from £10 to £12.

items.title: The Highway Litter Clearance and Cleaning (Transfer of Duties) Order 1991
The Vehicles (Charges for Release from Immobilisation Devices) Regulations 1991
The Misuse of Drugs (Licence Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Public Telecommunications System Designated (Cablevision Communications Company Limited) (Central Hertfordshire) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cablevision Communications Company Limited on 8th December 1990 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Central Hertfordshire area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 17th January 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cablevision Communications Company Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Central Hertfordshire public telecommunications systems when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Stock Transfer (Specified Securities) Order 1991
The Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fire Servicess (Appointments and Promotion) (Scotland) Regulations 1978 (as amended, in particular, by the Fire Services (Examinations) Regulations 1985 and by the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”)) which relate to the qualifications for appointment or promotion in a fire brigade.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No.11 and Savings) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 1991 provisions of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (in so far as they are not already in force) which make amendments and repeals, relating to allowances to members of local authorities and other bodies, relating to—

items.title: The Health Education Board for Scotland (Transfer of Officers) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 25th March 1991 provide for the transfer, on 1st April 1991, of those employed by the Common Services Agency for the Scottish Health Service in connection with health education, particularly in the Scottish Health Education Group, to the Health Education Board for Scotland.

items.title: The Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations which extend to Scotland only, require persons with custody or control of a crop being made into silage, of livestock slurry or of certain fuel oil to carry out works and take precautions and other steps for preventing pollution of waters which are controlled waters for the purposes of Part 11 of the Control of Pollution Act 1974.

items.title: The General Lighthouse Authorities (Beacons: Hyperbolic Systems) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the references to a beacon in Part XI of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 to include equipment and facilities in the United Kingdom which form part of a position fixing system known as the Loran C system so long as the United Kingdom is party to an international agreement concerning the establishment and operation of the system. The equipment and facilities would thus become subject to the superintendence and management of the General Lighthouse Authorities if the system were introduced into the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Finance Act 1985 (Interest on Tax) (Prescribed Rate) Order 1991
The Local Authorities (Members' Allowances) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision about the payment of allowances to members of local authorities and certain related bodies.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Reductions) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations correct an error in the formula set out in paragraph of regulation 22 of the Personal Community Charge (Reductions) (England) Regulations 1991. The substituted formula is relevant for the purposes of the calculation of certain reductions under Part V (persons in sheltered accommodation) of the Regulations.

items.title: The Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations replace, as respects England, the provisions of the Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Finance) Regulations 1990. The 1990 Regulations are amended by these Regulations so that they continue to apply to Wales.

items.title: The Attachment of Earnings (Employer’s Deduction) Order 1991
The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1991.
The National Health Service Trusts (Consultation before Establishment) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the consultation to be carried out by a Health Board or by the Common Services Agency for the Scottish Health Service before the Secretary of State for Scotland makes an Order under section 12A(1) of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 establishing a National Health Service trust in Scotland.

items.title: The Islwyn (Pontllanfraith and Blackwood Communities) Order 1991
The Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (Variation of Schedule) Order 1991
items.description: This Order, which applies throughout Great Britain, varies Schedule 5 to the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981, which lists animals protected under that Act.

items.title: The Eastbourne, Newcastle and Gateshead and North Surrey (Pipelaying and Other Works)(Codes of Practice) Order 1991
The Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) Regulation 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotions) Regulations 1978 (“the 1978 Regulations” (as amended, in particular, by the Fire Services (Examinations) Regulations 1985 and by the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”)) which relate to the qualifications for appointment or promotion in a fire brigade.

items.title: The Buckinghamshire County Council Downhead Park to Willen Park (Canal Bridge) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1991
The Imported Food (Peruvian Foodstuffs) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the importation into Great Britain of Peruvian food, (regulation 2(1)) exceptions to the prohibition are provided (regulation 2(2) and Schedules 1 and 2). The provisions of the Food Safety Act 1990 relating to the commission of offences by corporations, or as a result of another person’s fault, documentary evidence and the defence of due diligence, are applied to the offence of importing Peruvian food in breach of the Regulations (regulations 2(3) and 3). The Regulations are to be enforced by specified food authorities, port health authorities and port local authorities (regulation 4).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Refunds for Bad Depts) Regulations 1991
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Britannia Cablesystems Wirral Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Britannia Cablesystems Wirral Limited on 16th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Metropolitan Borough of Wirral. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 6th March 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Britannia Cablesystems Wirral Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the order comes into force on 3rd April 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Newport Cablevision) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Newport Cablevision on 23rd January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Newport (Gwent) area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 6th February 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Newport Cablevision will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 3rd April 1991.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Tayside Cable Systems Limited) (Dundee) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Tayside Cable Systems Limited on 17th January 1991 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Dundee area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 5th March 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Tayside Cable Systems Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of its Dundee public telecommunication systems when the order comes into force on 2nd April 1991.

items.title: The Oxfordshire (District Boundaries) Order 1991
The Buckinghamshire County Council H8 Standing Way (V8-V10) Dualling (Canal Bridge) Scheme 1990 Confirmation Instrument 1991
The Insolvency (Amendment) Regulations 1991
The Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) Regulations 1991
The Local Government (Non-Domestic District Rates and District Community Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulation 1991
items.description: These regulations amend the Local Government (Non-Domestic Rates and District Community Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1963) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Listed Bodies) Order 1991
items.description: This Order contains the list of the names of each body which is not a recognised body within section 214(2) (a) or (b) of the Education Reform Act 1988 but which either—

items.title: The Education (Recognised Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Local Authority Stocks and Bonds (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 4(1) of the Local Authority Stocks and Bonds (Scotland) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”) by adding at sub-paragraph (c) terms of repayment of bonds issued by local authorities.

items.title: The Enterprise (Scotland) Consequential Amendments Order 1991
items.description: The Enterprise and New Towns (Scotland) Act 1990 created in Scotland two new public bodies to be known as Scottish Enterprise and Highlands and Islands Enterprise. As from 1st April 1991, these bodies will assume responsibility for training arrangements in Scotland previously made by the Secretary of State for Employment under the Employment and Training Act 1973 (c. 50). This Order consequentially amends references in subordinate legislation to the Secretary of State and that 1973 Act so as to include, as from 1st April 1991, references to Scottish Enterprise and Highlands and Islands Enterprise and to the Enterprise and New Towns (Scotland) Act 1990.

items.title: The National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 (Commencement No. 7) Order 1991
The Disablement Services Authority (Consequential Provisions) Order 1991
The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Great Britain, further amend the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”), which implement part of Council Directive 75/268/EEC (O.J. No. L128, 19.5.75, p.1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas, and comply with Articles 13 to 15 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1), on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended.

items.title: The Sugar Beet (Research and Education) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the carrying into effect for the year 1991-92 of the programme, as set out in the Schedule, of research and education in matters affecting the growing of home-grown beet.

items.title: The Local Authorities Etc. (Allowance) (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provision about the payment of allowances to members of local authorities and joint boards and certain related bodies.

items.title: The Central and Tayside Regions and Clackmannan and Perth and Kinross Districts (Crook of Devon and Rumbling Bridge) Boundaries Amendment Order 1991
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1991 three adjacent areas extending in total to approximately 251.5 hectares between Clackmannan District in Central Region and Perth and Kinross District in Tayside Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Transitional Provisions and Savings) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Occupational Pension Schemes (Transitional Provisions and Savings) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) in order to make further transitional provisions and savings in connection with the coming into force of section 11(3) to (6) of the Social Security Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”).

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) Order 1991
items.description: This Order amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by substituting new schedules for Schedules 1 and 2 to that Order (article 2 (a) and (b)). Schedule 1, which lists approved disinfectants, includes newly approved disinfectants, and Schedule 2 lists disinfectants which are now omitted from Schedule I but which may nevertheless continue to be used as approved disinfectants until 30th June 1991 (article 2 (c)).

items.title: The Poultry Flocks, Hatcheries and Processed Animal Protein (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1991
The National Health Service (Vocational Training) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health (Vocational Training) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”) which prescribe the medical experience which, under section 31 of the National Health Service Act 1977, a medical practitioner is required to have acquired, or to be exempt from the need to have acquired, before his name may be included in a list of general medical practitioners maintained under section 29 of that Act.

items.title: The United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1991
items.description: This Order provides for the establishment and constitution of a special health authority, to be known as the United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority, for the purpose of exercising on behalf of the Secretary of State functions relating to the transplantation of human organs and ancillary and related matters. These functions were formerly exercised by a specialised unit of the South Western Regional Health Authority known as the United Kingdom Transplant Service.

items.title: The United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations make provisions concerning the functions and membership of the United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority established by the United Kingdom Transplant Support Service Authority (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1991. They include provisions relating to the functions of the Authority (regulation 2 and the Schedule), the appointment of members (regulation 3), tenure of office of members (regulation 4), eligibility of chairman and members for re-appointment (regulation 5), filling of casual vacancies (regulation 6), and termination of tenure of office (regulation 7).

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 9) Order 1991
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Education Reform Act 1988 relating to the admission of pupils to county and voluntary schools.

items.title: The Standard Community Charge (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the Standard Community Charge (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/2437) (“the principal Regulations”) with effect from 1st April 1991.

items.title: The Education (Variation of Standard Numbers for Primary Schools) Order 1991
The Education (Publication of Proposals for Reduction in Standard Number) Regulations 1991
The Petty Sessional Divisions (Powys) Order 1991
The Sea Fish Industry Authority (Levy) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 Confirmatory Order 1991
items.description: This Order confirms, after modification, Regulations made by the Sea Fish Industry Authority which amend the Sea Fish Industry Authority (Levy) Regulations 1988. The amending Regulations change most of the rates of the levy imposed by that Authority in respect of sea fish and sea fish products for the purpose of financing its activities. The old rates of levy are shown in italics in the revised Schedule to the new Regulations.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Scotland) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the non-domestic rate for the financial year 1991-92 for each local authority listed in the Schedule (regulation 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cheshire Cable Limited) Order 1991
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cheshire Cable Limited a licence on 31st January 1991 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in Chester and the districts of Ellesmere Port & Neston, Halton and Warrington. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 11th March 1991. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cheshire Cable Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 8th April 1991.

items.title: The Public Airport Companies (Capital Finance) (Amendment) Order 1991
The International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of the Agreement on the International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs and the Special Equipment used for such Carriage (ATP) of 1st September 1970 in the International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs Regulations 1985 to take account of a further amendment to the agreement.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Amendment) Order 1991
The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase Order 1991
The Statutory Sick Pay (Small Employers' Relief) Regulations 1991
items.description: This instrument contains only Regulations made consequential upon section 2 of the Statutory Sick Pay Act 1991 (c. 3). As it is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of that enactment (14th February 1991) it is exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24), Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from reference to the Social Security Advisory Committee and has not been so referred.

items.title: The State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations further amend the State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Regulations 1987. They make provision for calculations relating to state scheme premiums (other than contributions equivalent premiums) to be made by reference to a 20-year index number instead of by reference to a 25-year index number. They do this by amending regulations 1, 2 and 10 of those Regulations and by substituting a new Schedule for Schedule 7 to those Regulations.

items.title: The Caledonian MacBrayne Limited (Arinagour Pier) Harbour Revision Order 1990
items.description: This Order transfer to and vests in Caledonian MacBrayne Limited the Arinagour Pier undertaking on the Island of Coll together with all rights, interests and privileges presently vested in or used by Strathclde Regional Council in relation to that undertaking. Strathclyde Regional Council are the present undertakers at Arinagour by virtue of section 154 of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 which transferred the pier undertaking from Argyll County Council to them. Provision is made for all conveyances, contracts, agreements or deeds entered into by Strathclyde Regional Council in relation to that undertaking to remain binding and in full force against or in favour of the Company and for all legal or other proceedings in relation to that undertaking to be carried on with the substition of the Company as a party to the proceedings in lieu of Strathclyde Regional Council. This Order also provides that sections 202 and 203 of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 shall apply to byelaws for the pier undertaking made by the Company and that byelaws and regulations in force when this Order comes into operation shall continue in force.

items.title: The Community Charges and Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1991
The Income Tax (Employments) (No. 20) Regulations 1991
The Wireless Telegraphy (Television Licence Fees) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regu1ations consolidate the Regulations specified in Schedule 1. In addition to minor drafting amendments, these Regulations increase the fees payable on the issue of the television licences described in Schedules 2 and 3 and regulation 4, except the fees referred to in entry 7 of Part I of Schedule 2. There is also a re-organisation of the content of the Regulations.

items.title: The National Assistance (Charges for Accomodation) Amendment Regulations 1991
The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Amendment Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Capital Money) (Greater London) Order 1991
items.description: This Order re-enacts, with amendments, the Local Government Reorganisation (Capital Money) (Greater London) Order 1990.

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Community Charge Benefit (Subsidy) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations specify the particulars which are required to be provided by an authority to the Secretary of State on the making of claims for housing benefit subsidy and community charge benefit subsidy, the time within which claims for subsidy are to be made and the information and records to be produced to the Secretary of State in support of such claims.

items.title: The Common Council and New Successor Bodies (Chief Finance Officer) Regulations 1991
items.description: These Regulations apply to the Common Council in its capacity as a local authority, police authority or port health authority, to the South Yorkshire Pensions Authority and to the London Pensions Fund Authority.
